g.text: 💡 KEY INSIGHT: The Magic of Tensor Reshaping
Memory Layout: Why contiguous() is Required
Misconception: Iterating over heads one at a time
Reality: All heads compute in ONE batched operation

text.tspan: ✅ Single batched matmul for ALL heads
The 'heads' dimension becomes part of the batch dimension through reshaping.
This allows a SINGLE matrix multiplication to compute attention for ALL heads.
GPU sees: [batch × heads, seq, d_k] → processes all heads in parallel
The model 'thinks' it has 8 separate attention heads.
The implementation computes them as one big batched operation.
This is why transformers are so efficient on GPUs!
⚠️ .view() requires contiguous memory. After transpose, must call
